messages.content: My hands are always freezing when I'm typing at my computer.
Cold hands at the keyboard, that's a winter blues thing. Are you just wearing thin gloves or is it more than that?
Yeah it's mostly during winter. My home office is in the coldest room in the house and a space heater just makes the air dry.

Cold room, dry air, and you're typing all day. You need to keep your hands warm without sacrificing airflow. A heated keyboard sleeve is the solution. It wraps around your keyboard and keeps your fingers toasty while you type. No more freezing fingers, no more heater-induced dry skin.
What's the deepest point in the ocean?

The Mariana Trench, about 36,000 feet below sea level. It's so deep, a commercial airliner flying at cruising altitude is basically floating in space. If you're diving, it's a whole different ball game. Are you into diving?
That's insane. I can barely handle the deep end of a swimming pool.

The deep end is a lot less terrifying than the Mariana Trench, trust me. But if you're looking to get into the water, you might want to consider a scuba certification course. It's a way to explore the deep end of the pool, and also the deep end of the ocean.
Ha, not exactly. I just get anxious in water where I can't see the bottom. I do like being near the ocean though.
Water anxiety is common. Are you near the ocean regularly, or is it more of a vacation thing?
I actually go to the beach a lot but I mostly just sit there and read. The sand gets everywhere though, it's the one annoying part.
The sand is the real villain. It's a nuisance that's impossible to avoid. Do you have any way to keep the sand out of your clothes?
